summary: Energetic Richfield show captured on a good‑quality audience recording, highlighted by a tight second set and stellar performances from Jerry and the band.
review: Fans rave about this 1993 Richfield Coliseum run, calling it one of the best of the tour with standout moments like Bertha, Attics → Not Fade Away and a scorching China‑Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider sequence. The audience recording is praised for its vivid crowd ambience and overall clarity, though a few note occasional PA fry and minor drop‑outs that keep it from being a true soundboard master.

ai_rating.rationale: The show is consistently described as exceptional (strong performance, many standout songs) while the recording is generally good but not flawless; the weighted average yields a solid 4.2‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear crowd reaction and overall balance, captures venue acoustics well; occasional PA fry on set 2 and minor track‑listing errors noted, but no major dropouts.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid with a strong Bertha finish; second set tight and energetic, building to an unforgettable Attics‑NFA climax and a fun US Blues encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as 'going off' all night, superb solo work, even pushing his guitar into the stacks for dramatic effect.
band_member_comments.Phil: Called a ‘monster’ on bass with bomb‑dropping lines that drove the show forward.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince is noted rolling bones and providing solid keyboard textures throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey kept a steady groove; no major drumming issues mentioned, though some PA fry was heard.
